title: O for a Thousand Tongues to Sing
author: Charles Wesley
story: Wesley wrote this on the first anniversary of his conversion. The title came from Peter Böhler's words: 'Had I a thousand tongues, I would praise Him with them all.' It opens most Methodist hymnals.

lines: O for a thousand tongues to sing My great Redeemer's praise, The glories of my God and King, The triumphs of His grace
Hear Him, ye deaf
His praise, ye dumb, 7
In Christ your Head, you then shall know, 8
Glory to God, and praise and love 9
On this glad day the glorious Sun 10
Sudden expired the legal strife, 11
Then with my heart I first believed, 12
I felt my Lord's atoning blood 13
I found and owned His promise true, 14
Look unto Him, ye nations, own 15
See all your sins on Jesus laid: 16
Awake from guilty nature's sleep, 17
Harlots and publicans and thieves 18
Murderers and all ye hellish crew, 19
With me, your chief, ye then shall know, Shall feel your sins forgiven
Anticipate your heaven below, And own that love is heaven
My gracious Master and my God, Assist me to proclaim, To spread through all the earth abroad The honors of Thy name
Your loosened tongues employ
Shall feel your sins forgiven
Be ever, ever given, Of Righteousness arose
'Twas then I ceased to grieve
Believed with faith divine, Close to my soul applied
Ascertained of my part, Your God, ye fallen race
The Lamb of God was slain, And Christ shall give you light, In holy triumph join
Ye sons of lust and pride, 19
With me, your chief, ye then shall know, Shall feel your sins forgiven
Anticipate your heaven below, And own that love is heaven
Jesus
the name that charms our fears, That bids our sorrows cease
'Tis music in the sinner's ears, 'Tis life, and health, and peace

Ye blind, behold your Savior come, Anticipate your heaven below, By saints below and saints above, On my benighted soul He shone My second, real, living life Power with the Holy Ghost received Me, me He loved, the Son of God, My pardon passed in heaven I knew Look, and be saved through faith alone, His soul was once an offering made Cast all your sins into the deep, Saved is the sinner that believes Believe the Savior died for you
With me, your chief, ye then shall know, Shall feel your sins forgiven
Anticipate your heaven below, And own that love is heaven
He breaks the pow'r of canceled sin, He sets the prisoner free
His blood can make the foulest clean, His blood availed for me
And leap, ye lame, for joy
And own that love is heaven
The church in earth and heaven
And filled it with repose
I then began to live
To call the Savior mine
For me, for me He died
When written on my heart
Be justified by grace
For every soul of man
And wash the �thiop white
From crimes as great as mine
For me the Savior died
With me, your chief, ye then shall know, Shall feel your sins forgiven
Anticipate your heaven below, And own that love is heaven
He speaks, and, listening to His voice, New life the dead receive, The mournful, broken hearts rejoice, The humble poor believe
With me, your chief, ye then shall know, Shall feel your sins forgiven
Anticipate your heaven below, And own that love is heaven
